reasoning: Chrome has maintained a massive market share lead (around 65% globally across all platforms) for many years, far ahead of the second-most popular browser (Safari, around 18-20%). The knowledge cutoff is November 9, 2025, less than two months before the end of the year. Market share metrics for web browsers change very slowly, making it virtually impossible for any other browser to overtake Chrome in such a short timeframe without an unprecedented structural change (e.g., Chrome being globally banned and immediately replaced everywhere). Given the huge margin, the very short time horizon, and the community market estimate of 0.97, the probability is overwhelmingly high.
